$comment: Brain Protocol v2 controlled vocabulary (BP-01 §8-§10) plus the subtype→sensitivity starter table (BP-01 §9, semantics in BP-07 §2.3). This is a data document, not a JSON Schema. Mapping tables (BP-01 §11) are the only extension valve; new base terms land by MINOR version through the brain-protocol repository process. Unknown terms received without a mapping pass through opaquely and are never silently aliased.

action_verbs.$comment: Action verbs (subtype on actions) are not enumerated by the base vocabulary: they are named by the capability they invoke (book, send_message, entity.merge, …) and are validated against the grant matrix (BP-03), not this registry. (BP-01 §10.4)

registered_scopes.audience: all adult members of the space

visibility.unknown_scope_rule: A receiver encountering an unknown scope MUST treat the record as private — fail closed, never open (BP-01 §8, CD-6).

sensitivity_classes.name: Ambient
sensitivity_classes.wire: live-query preferred; never stored by the receiver by default

sensitivity_classes.name: Personal
sensitivity_classes.wire: elevated grant cell + step-up confirm + JWE end-to-end

sensitivity_classes.name: Sealed / identity
sensitivity_classes.wire: never syncs — reference-only pointers cross the wire

sensitivity_starter.$comment: Starter default by subtype (BP-01 §9). Emitters MAY stamp higher than the default; receivers MAY upgrade and MUST NOT downgrade (BP-07 §2.2). Edges carry no class of their own by subtype: an edge inherits the stricter of its endpoints (BP-01 §5.6).
contextual_overrides: document defaults S1; clinical documents are S2; identity documents (passport, birth-certificate, account-number payloads) are S3
observation defaults S1; health observations are S2
credentials and vault items are S3; S3 never syncs — reference-only pointers cross the wire (BP-07 §2.4)

needs_human_reasons.meaning: the exchange crosses an S2/S3 boundary without the required consent or step-up (BP-07)
entity resolution or extraction below threshold; a human must look
the grant's mode is propose-only and the proposal needs the receiving side's human
the two systems' records conflict and an action depends on which is right
the authority policy or a protocol floor (BP-08 §6) requires a human regardless
semantic dead end — an unmapped subtype or predicate the action depends on (CD-6)
